message:
style(pt-core): apply Clippy lints and idiomatic Rust improvements

Minor code quality improvements across action and inference modules:

## Action module (executor.rs, prechecks.rs, supervisor.rs):

**executor.rs:**
- Remove unnecessary `ref` in `if let Some(ref provider)` pattern
  The pattern `if let Some(ref x)` can be simplified to `if let Some(x)`
  when borrowing is sufficient (Clippy: needless_borrowed_reference)

**prechecks.rs:**
- Replace `.min(200).max(10)` with `.clamp(10, 200)` for clarity
  The clamp method is more readable and expresses intent directly
- Simplify format! macro to direct string concatenation where the format
  string has no interpolation (reduces macro overhead)

**supervisor.rs:**
- Replace manual Default implementation with derive(Default) on
  SupervisorParameters struct - all fields already have Option or bool
  types with sensible defaults (None, false)
- Use struct update syntax `{ field: value, ..Default::default() }` instead
  of creating struct then mutating - more idiomatic and clearer intent

## Inference module (bocpd.rs):

- Add underscore separator to numeric literal: -1259.139... → -1_259.139...
  Improves readability of Lanczos approximation coefficient in ln_gamma
  function without changing the value

These changes are purely stylistic and do not alter any logic or behavior.
All changes follow standard Rust idioms recommended by Clippy and the
Rust API Guidelines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
